question: Among these labeled cases, what per-case signals distinguish the FAIL cases (false 'Yes' on an absent object) from the PASS cases? Propose composite signals as deterministic recipes over the available numeric columns.
observations: 121 labeled cases: 25 FAIL (false 'Yes') vs 96 PASS (20.7% fail rate).
prompt_sensitivity present for 85 cases; the 7 relative_attention signals present for only 22 cases (heavy missingness).

visual_plan.name: class_balance
visual_plan.question: How imbalanced are the labeled outcomes?
visual_plan.rationale: Counts establish the base rate so later separations are read against imbalance.

visual_plan.name: top_discriminators
visual_plan.question: Which numeric signals separate FAIL from PASS most?
visual_plan.rationale: Ranked |standardized mean diff| surfaces candidates without assuming a model.

visual_plan.name: group_means
visual_plan.question: In which direction does each signal shift for FAIL?
visual_plan.rationale: Side-by-side means show the sign of separation; pre-aggregated to avoid row dumps.

visual_plan.name: attention_box_by_outcome
visual_plan.question: Do attention distributions differ for FAIL vs PASS?
visual_plan.rationale: Box+strip shows spread and overlap that a bar-of-means hides at small n.

visual_plan.name: failrate_by_focus_share
visual_plan.question: Does lower attention focus raise failure risk?
visual_plan.rationale: Ordered bins show the risk trend without assuming linearity.

visual_plan.name: corr_heatmap
visual_plan.question: Which signals are redundant vs independent?
visual_plan.rationale: Correlation guards against double-counting collinear attention features.

visual_plan.name: scatter_top_attention_pair
visual_plan.question: Do the two best signals jointly separate FAIL?
visual_plan.rationale: Colored scatter reveals interaction/region structure a 1-D view misses.

chart_readings.reading: PASS dominates (96) over FAIL (25); ~21% base fail rate.
chart_readings.do_not_infer: Imbalance alone says nothing about which signals drive failures.

chart_readings.reading: Bars rank signals by standardized FAIL-vs-PASS mean gap; attention focus/edge/entropy signals tend to top prompt_sensitivity.
chart_readings.do_not_infer: A large gap on n=22 attention cases is not a confirmed or significant effect.

chart_readings.reading: Shows the direction of each shift (e.g. FAIL = lower focus_share / higher edge_mass).
chart_readings.do_not_infer: Mean direction does not establish a threshold or a causal mechanism.

chart_readings.reading: FAIL boxes shift toward diffuse/peripheral attention but overlap PASS substantially.
chart_readings.do_not_infer: Overlap means no clean separator; small n makes whiskers unstable.

chart_readings.reading: Fail rate is higher in the lowest focus_share bins.
chart_readings.do_not_infer: The binned trend is descriptive, not a fitted dose-response.

chart_readings.reading: Several attention features are mutually correlated (focus_share, top1_share, entropy).
chart_readings.do_not_infer: Collinearity does not tell which feature is the true driver.

chart_readings.reading: FAIL points cluster toward the diffuse/peripheral corner of the two top signals.
chart_readings.do_not_infer: Clustering on the labeled set may not hold out-of-sample.

dashboard_storyboard.title: Problem Setting
dashboard_storyboard.summary: 121 labeled cases where FAIL = false 'Yes' on an absent object; per-case M1 signals are prompt sensitivity and 7 relative-attention features (attention present for ~22 cases).
items: FAIL means the model hallucinated a present object that is absent.
Base fail rate 21% (25/121); attention features missing for 99 cases.

dashboard_storyboard.title: Analysis
dashboard_storyboard.summary: Rank each numeric signal by standardized FAIL-vs-PASS separation, view distributions and binned fail-rate curves, and check signal collinearity.
items: Method: |standardized mean diff| -> chart:top_discriminators -> attention focus/edge signals lead.
Method: binned fail-rate -> chart:failrate_by_focus_share -> risk rises at low focus.
Method: correlation -> chart:corr_heatmap -> attention features are partly redundant.

dashboard_storyboard.title: Hypotheses & Artifacts
dashboard_storyboard.summary: Composite deterministic recipes proposed as predictors for held-out confirmation.
items: M3: ungrounded attention (diffuse and/or peripheral) drives false positives.
M4: test the diffuse_and_peripheral recipe on a held-out attention-present split.
M5: if confirmed, an attention-grounding intervention should reduce false 'Yes'.

claims.text: Diffuse, low-focus attention is a descriptive correlate of false-positive (FAIL) cases.
claims.interpretation: When attention is spread out rather than concentrated, the model is more likely to assert an absent object is present.
claims.do_not_infer: Not causal and based on only ~22 attention-instrumented cases.

claims.text: Edge/peripheral attention concentration co-occurs with FAIL cases.
claims.interpretation: Attention leaking to image borders accompanies ungrounded 'Yes' answers.
claims.do_not_infer: Direction of effect unconfirmed; edge_mass correlates with other signals.

claims.text: Prompt-insensitive answers (zero sensitivity) are a sanity-check correlate of FAIL.
claims.interpretation: Answers unchanged across prompt phrasings suggest an ungrounded prior.
claims.do_not_infer: Sensitivity is 0 for most cases, so discrimination is weak.

candidate_signals.name: diffuse_attention
candidate_signals.description: Low focus_share with high entropy = attention spread over no real object; the model 'sees' the absent object everywhere and answers Yes.
candidate_signals.suggested_test: Compare held-out fail rate of diffuse vs concentrated attention cases.

candidate_signals.name: peripheral_attention
candidate_signals.description: High edge_mass + large center_offset = attention drawn to image borders rather than a grounded object location.
candidate_signals.suggested_test: Held-out fail rate for peripheral vs central attention.

candidate_signals.name: prompt_insensitive
candidate_signals.description: Zero sensitivity = the model gives the same answer regardless of prompt phrasing, a hallmark of an ungrounded prior 'Yes'.
candidate_signals.suggested_test: Held-out fail rate for prompt-insensitive vs sensitive cases.

candidate_signals.name: diffuse_and_peripheral
candidate_signals.description: Interaction: unfocused attention that also leaks to edges is the strongest ungrounded-attention pattern.
candidate_signals.suggested_test: Held-out fail rate for the conjunction vs the rest.

recommended_confirmatory_tests: Confirm the diffuse_attention and diffuse_and_peripheral recipes on a held-out split via the host's multiplicity-aware core.
Collect attention features for the remaining cases to remove small-n / selection limits.
M5 surgery: apply attention re-grounding to high-edge_mass cases and re-measure the false-positive rate.
Check whether missingness of attention features itself predicts FAIL.
caveats: Attention signals exist for only 22/121 cases; all attention findings are small-n.
Recipe thresholds use medians of the present-data subset (data-driven) -> mild optimism; the host should confirm on a held-out split.
Class imbalance (~21% FAIL) inflates apparent separations; fail-rate curves are sparse per bin.
prompt_sensitivity is 0 for most cases, giving little spread.
marginal per-case signal verdicts are CI-based at alpha and NOT e-BH FDR-corrected across the signal family (unpaired test has no e-value)
critique: Double-dipping risk: medians for thresholds are computed on the same rows used to report separation; recipes are emitted so the host can re-evaluate out-of-sample.
Heavy missingness in attention features could be non-random (which cases get instrumented), a possible selection confound.
Standardized mean diff assumes roughly comparable spread; with n=22 the pooled SD is unstable.
Probe/prompt_sensitivity fields are treated as sanity-check evidence, not primary explanatory findings.
conclusion: Standalone statistical analysis found a supported signal. Statistically supported: signal 'peripheral_attention' vs FAIL: [clustered bootstrap (unpaired)] effect=+0.5789 (B>A) CI=+0.0789..+0.8947 -> REJECT H0
